ballgown.champagne: She's an adult in formal wear, so she should be offered champagne, but she wasn't.
ballgown.canapes: Formal guests are offered canapés too, but she wasn't.
ballgown.letIn: Anyone in formal wear should be let in, but she wasn't.
ballgown.notTurnedAway: A guest in formal wear should NOT be turned away.

tuxedo.champagne: He's 18, which counts as an adult, so he should be offered champagne, but he wasn't.
tuxedo.canapes: Formal guests are offered canapés too, but he wasn't.
tuxedo.letIn: Anyone in formal wear should be let in, but he wasn't.
tuxedo.notTurnedAway: A guest in formal wear should NOT be turned away.

dress.canapes: She's in smart wear, so she should be offered canapés, but she wasn't.
dress.noChampagne: Champagne is for formal wear, so a smart guest shouldn't be offered it.
dress.letIn: A guest in smart wear should be let in, but she wasn't.
dress.notTurnedAway: A guest in smart wear should NOT be turned away.

suit.canapes: He's in smart wear, so he should be offered canapés, but he wasn't.
suit.noChampagne: Champagne is for formal wear, so a smart guest shouldn't be offered it.
suit.letIn: A guest in smart wear should be let in, but he wasn't.
suit.notTurnedAway: A guest in smart wear should NOT be turned away.

formalTeen.letIn: Anyone in formal wear is let in, whatever their age, but she wasn't.
formalTeen.canapes: Formal guests are offered canapés whatever their age, but she wasn't.
formalTeen.noChampagne: She's under 18, so she shouldn't be offered champagne. Champagne is for adults only.
formalTeen.notTurnedAway: A guest in formal wear should NOT be turned away.

smartTeen.canapes: Smart wear gets canapés whatever their age, but he wasn't offered any.
smartTeen.noChampagne: Champagne is for formal wear, so a smart guest shouldn't be offered it.
smartTeen.letIn: A guest in smart wear should be let in, but he wasn't.
smartTeen.notTurnedAway: A guest in smart wear should NOT be turned away.

casualChildListed.letIn: An under-18 on the guest list is allowed in, even in casual clothes, but they weren't.
casualChildListed.notTurnedAway: An under-18 on the guest list should NOT be turned away.
casualChildListed.noChampagne: They're not in formal wear, so no champagne.
casualChildListed.noCanapes: They're not in formal or smart wear, so no canapés.

casualChildUnlisted.turnedAway: An under-18 in casual clothes who isn't on the guest list should be turned away.
casualChildUnlisted.notLetIn: An under-18 in casual clothes who isn't on the guest list should NOT be let in.
casualChildUnlisted.noChampagne: They're not in formal wear, so no champagne.
casualChildUnlisted.noCanapes: They're not in formal or smart wear, so no canapés.

casualAdultListed.turnedAway: The guest-list exception is only for under-18s, so an adult in casual clothes is turned away even if they're on the list.
casualAdultListed.notLetIn: An adult in casual clothes should NOT be let in, even if they're on the guest list.
casualAdultListed.noChampagne: They're not in formal wear, so no champagne.
casualAdultListed.noCanapes: They're not in formal or smart wear, so no canapés.

codeQuality.combinesConditions: This challenge is about combining conditions. Try using what you've learned in the previous lesson.

checkDressCode.name: Enforce the door policy
checkDressCode.description: Check each person's outfit, age, and guest-list status, then apply the rules. Formal or smart clothes get in with canapés. Adults in formal clothes also get champagne. Under-18s in other clothes get in only if they're on the guest list. Everyone else is turned away.

ballgown.name: Ballgown
ballgown.description: A woman in her 60s wearing a ballgown. She gets champagne and canapés, and is let in.

tuxedo.name: Tuxedo
tuxedo.description: A man in a tuxedo who has just turned 18. He gets champagne and canapés, and is let in.

dress.name: Dress
dress.description: A woman in a dress. She's let in with canapés.

suit.name: Suit
suit.description: A man in a suit. He's let in with canapés.

formalTeen.name: Child in a ballgown
formalTeen.description: A 13-year-old in a ballgown. She's let in with canapés, but is too young for champagne.

smartTeen.name: Teenager in a suit
smartTeen.description: A 15-year-old in a suit. He's let in with canapés, regardless of age.

casualChildListed.name: Child in denim, on the list
casualChildListed.description: A 15-year-old in denim who's on the guest list. They're let in.

casualChildUnlisted.name: Child in a tracksuit, not on the list
casualChildUnlisted.description: A 15-year-old in a tracksuit who isn't on the guest list. They're turned away.

casualAdultListed.name: Adult in denim, on the list
casualAdultListed.description: A 30-year-old in denim who's on the guest list. They're still turned away.

whereToStart.question: Where do I start?
whereToStart.answer: Fetch the outfit and the age once each and store them in variables, e.g. `let outfit = getOutfit()`. Then you can build all your conditions from those variables.

groupingOutfits.question: Several outfits lead to the same outcome. Do I need a branch for each one?
groupingOutfits.answer: No. You can check for more than one value in a single condition using `||` (or), so a group of outfits can share one branch.

champagneRule.question: How do I decide who gets champagne?
champagneRule.answer: Champagne is its own decision: offer it when the person is an adult AND wearing something formal. That combines `&&` (and) with `||` (or), so put parentheses around the 'or' part to group it correctly.

canapesRule.question: Who gets canapés and gets let in?
canapesRule.answer: Everyone dressed up does, which is any of the four formal or smart outfits. You can list them all in a single condition with `||`.

everyoneElse.question: What about everyone else?
everyoneElse.answer: The only other people allowed in are under-18s on the guest list, which needs two things to both be true (`&&`). Finish with a plain `else` that turns everyone else away.

previousLessonOperator.question: I don't know what it means by "what I learned in the previous lesson".
previousLessonOperator.answer: You need to use the "and" operator that Jeremy taught in the previous video to combine conditionals. Re-read the concept page if you're not sure how to use it.

getOutfit.description: Returns the **outfit** the person is wearing (e.g. `"ballgown"`, `"suit"`, `"denim"`).

getAge.description: Returns the **age** of the person waiting at the door.

onGuestList.description: Returns `true` if the person is on tonight's guest list, otherwise `false`.

offerChampagne.description: Offers the person a glass of champagne.

offerCanapes.description: Offers the person some canapés (salmon tartare).

letIn.description: Lets the person into the venue.

turnAway.description: Turns the person away from the venue.
